name: Art
tagline: Four resident studios. The walls change every quarter.
cardBody: Four resident studios show on the big nights, sell from the bar, run workshops on quiet ones. The walls change every quarter.
problem: Working visual artists in Nanyuki sell into Nairobi galleries that take 50% and pay slowly, or they sell direct to tourists at curio prices. There is no permanent showing space, no studio infrastructure, and no buyer-development programme inside the town. The result: the talent leaves for Nairobi or stops practising. The audience never sees what is actually being made next door.
approach: Downtown hosts four resident studios on-site, rent-free, with one published exhibition per resident per quarter. Sales happen at the bar at gallery margin. A quarterly rotation on the main wall gives every resident a public premiere; the bar is the entry point for buyers who would never set foot in a gallery.

programmes.name: Resident studio programme
programmes.description: Four studios on-site, year-long contracts, rent-free in exchange for one public showing and four open-studio nights per year.

programmes.name: Quarterly wall rotation
programmes.description: The main gallery wall changes every twelve weeks. One resident takes the wall, opens with a Friday-night preview, sells from the bar.

programmes.name: Open-studio nights
programmes.description: Once a quarter, all four studios open to the public. Residents are present, work is for sale, prices are published on the door.

programmes.name: Buyer-development
programmes.description: A short list of returning collectors gets a preview email before each new wall goes up. First-purchase support for under-thirty-fives in the town.

outcomes.target: Four resident studios occupied. Four wall rotations completed. First open-studio night.
Second gallery space opens. Sales pipeline tracked, residents earn from work shown at Downtown.
Annual group show co-curated with [Partner: Nairobi gallery]. Permanent-collection acquisitions begin.
Festival edition includes a dedicated visual-art week.
Permanent Downtown collection of [N] works on rotating display. International residency hosts two visiting artists.

partners: [Partner: British Council Kenya, visual arts grant]
